featured.title: Berlin Observation Decks: Which High View Is Actually Worth Paying For in 2026
featured.label: Newest viewpoint guide
featured.summary: Berlin has five public high viewpoints, from the free Reichstag dome to the 203 m TV Tower deck, and they are not equal on price, height or booking. Here is what each one costs in 2026, how you get up, and which Berlin observation deck I would actually send you to.
featured.alt: Warm evening view over central Berlin with the TV Tower rising above the rooftops and the Spree river

miniPosts.title: Tiergarten Berlin: What's Actually Worth Seeing in the City's Biggest Park
miniPosts.summary: Tiergarten is 210 hectares of woodland, lakes and formal garden sitting right between the Brandenburg Gate and Zoologischer Garten station, open around the clock and free to walk into. Here is what's actually worth seeing, the WWII story behind why the trees are so young, and how much time to give it.
miniPosts.alt: A cyclist crossing an ornamental stone bridge over a calm waterway in Berlin's Tiergarten, framed by spring blossoms

miniPosts.title: Currency Exchange in Berlin: Where to Change Money (and Where Not To) in 2026
miniPosts.summary: Most visitors do not need to exchange cash in Berlin at all. A local guide to getting euros without the hidden margin: bank ATMs, the airport-desk trap, the dynamic-conversion question, and how much cash to actually carry.
miniPosts.alt: Close-up of euro banknotes, a fifty and a twenty, the cash most visitors think they need to exchange before arriving in Berlin

miniPosts.title: U-Bahn vs S-Bahn in Berlin: What's the Difference?
miniPosts.summary: A blue U, a green S and a row of numbers on the same wall. A local guide to the U-Bahn vs S-Bahn difference: which is which, which to take, and why one ticket covers both.
miniPosts.alt: Berlin S-Bahn train on the red-brick Stadtbahn viaduct curving past the domes and rooftops of the city centre near Hackescher Markt

miniPosts.title: Air Conditioning in Berlin: Do Hotels Have It, and How to Stay Cool (2026)
miniPosts.summary: A Berlin guide's honest take on air conditioning in Berlin: why so few places have it, how to check before you book, and how to keep a no-AC room liveable in a summer heatwave.
miniPosts.alt: A classic Berlin Altbau room in summer with two tall open casement windows, sheer curtains, potted plants on the sill, an old cast-iron radiator, herringbone parquet floor and a small white electric fan by the window, warm afternoon sunlight across the floor

posts.title: Gendarmenmarkt: A Local Guide to Berlin's Most Beautiful Square (2026)
posts.summary: Two of Gendarmenmarkt's three landmarks are called cathedrals, and neither one is. A local guide to Berlin's most beautiful square: what each building really is, the free exhibition inside the German Cathedral, and whether the French tower climb is worth it.
posts.alt: Wide view across Gendarmenmarkt in Berlin, with the Konzerthaus on the left, the Schiller monument in the middle, and the French Cathedral with its green domed tower on the right

posts.title: Markthalle Neun: Berlin's Best Food Market and Street Food Thursday (2026)
posts.summary: Same market hall, completely different day. A local guide to Markthalle Neun in Kreuzberg: when Street Food Thursday runs, which day has the Big Market, and why you should never turn up on a Sunday.
posts.alt: The green cast-iron and glass interior of Markthalle Neun in Berlin-Kreuzberg, with hanging black-and-white portraits and food stalls under an arched roof

posts.title: Where to Watch the 2026 World Cup in Berlin (No, There's No Brandenburg Gate Fan Mile This Year)
posts.summary: The 2026 World Cup is in North America, and Berlin has no Brandenburg Gate fan mile this year. Here is where to actually watch: free screens, beer gardens and bars, with every kick-off in Berlin time.
posts.alt: A summer evening football public viewing in Berlin with a relaxed crowd, outdoor screen and TV Tower skyline
